metadata.title:
Low FREIGHT Rates


NOT TO BE EXTENDED

pages.raw_text:
SEATTLE, March al. d. Farrell,
assistant to the president of the
Great Northern railway and president
Of the Northern Steamship company.
arrived yesterday from the east


In an interview Mr.. Farrell states
that the Great Northern will not ex-
tend to ordinary commerce the rates
made for the transportation of gov
eminent supplies from Chicago to
Seattle.


1.-The fear which has been expressed
from outside sources that Seattle
jebbers would be injured is extremely
strained, and evidently comes from
people who wish the local men would
think So" said Mr.. Farrell.


Seattle wanted the bulk of the
government transport business, and
in order to get it the Great Northern
made a rate which is far below what
the southern lines can touch, and it
is from these sources that the great
anxiety for Seattle jobbers has come.


'"The big steamships of the North
ern Steamship company will soon be
around plying from Seattle to the
